title: 1.0 out of 5 stars
date: Reviewed in the United States on September 17, 2025
body: Think this cutter will be a good tool for making stickers? Think again... - The business model is to sell you TACK MATS and BLADES, not printers. I can't overstate how much of a piece of junk this $500 paperweight is. I'm on my third tack mat in just ~ 10x 8.5x11 uses. The first one, they replaced at no charge. These tack mats are total trash. They are too sticky out of the gate and when you use the factory provided scraper to remove excess, it pulls off the adhesive with your backing. Even their test cardstock project gets stuck to their tackmat. Then, they force you to use their tackmats presumably by weight and QR validation on the header so you have no alternative other than to beg to pay $30 to Brother for another smack in the face. - The build quality: hours after unboxing the rubber bumper on the door decided to fall off. The interface is painfully slow. The touch screen is shoddy at best. To save manufacturing costs (presumably) they decided to make the telescoping trays intentionally too short so the tack mat would bend slightly and your sticker would come unstuck, making you think you need to buy a new tack mat. - No returns. Not happy with it? Realize how much of a financial pit this piece of plastic is? Not our problem. JUNK. BUYER BEWARE.

title: 4.0 out of 5 stars
date: Reviewed in the United States on September 17, 2025
body: Buenos días, recién pudimos configurar la máquina ayer, porque lamentablemente nos llegó sin el tapete de corte, solo venía en la caja el tapete de escaneo, también faltaron las tarjetas para habilitar el contenido extra que debería venir también en la caja, sobre el material para pruebas que viene con el producto para probarlo con distintos materiales, sólo vinieron dos vinilos, fuera de ésos detalles finalmente pudimos ponerlo a prueba y nos gustó mucho, una muy buena experiencia de trabajo, con una interfaz lo suficientemente intuitiva como para poder llegar al corte o escaneo que se necesita para distintas tareas, ahora sólo nos queda seguir aprendiendo para poder sacarle el mayor partido a una máquina que por lo práctica que es, lamentamos no conocerla antes! Good morning, we were only able to configure the machine yesterday, because unfortunately it arrived without the cutting mat, only the scanning mat came in the box, the cards were also missing to enable the extra content that should also come in the box, on the test material that comes with the product to test it with different materials, only two vinyls came in the box, apart from those details we were finally able to put it to the test and we really liked it, a very good work experience, with an interface intuitive enough to be able to get to the cutting or scanning that is needed for different tasks, now we just have to keep learning in order to get the most out of a machine that because of how practical it is, we regret not knowing it before!

title: 2.0 out of 5 stars
date: Reviewed in the United States on September 14, 2025
body: I bought this printer a long while back. Had no issues with it. That all changed today. I have been trying as long as i have had this printer to cut stickers. Could never understand despite how many videos I watched why my Offset button was greyed out. I discovered after much intense research that you have to purchase a Print n Cut activation card just to create the registration marks the printer has to read and to create the offset the printer has to have in order to make the printer cut images as stickers. You then also have to buy a Printer Sticker starter kit activation card to create stickers. Without those two activation cards the printer will NOT allow you to print and cut stickers. Imagine my aggravation when i discovered this only to also discover that Brother discontinued both those activation cards. So i would have been just as well off keeping my Cricut Maker cutting machine. This was a terrible waste of money.

It needs its branded mat in order to properly read your images to cut and its smart technology. My only cons about it is that I can't turn the brightness down on the screen, and its sort of a huge machine. Other than that, I'm happy and its pretty much perfect, thank you.

title: 1.0 out of 5 stars
date: Reviewed in the United States on June 14, 2025
body: I own a small shop where I sell stickers. As demand grows, I looked for options to 'cut' down on the cutting times and after seeing a few comments on Reddit, this item was mentioned a few times. The only device that can scan your stickers? Where do I sign up? Here is my experience with this product. Pros: • It does scan fairly well. • Next day delivery was nice and it came undamaged. Cons: • While this might work well for simple stickers, if you are only using the scan option and not the canvas website it can't easily handle making cut lines for complex and multi colored stickers. I ended up having to send the scan to the canvas website and manually making my own cut lines, which significantly increased the time it took to cut and made the whole point of ordering this scanning machine pointless. • The cuts were always off and no amount of trouble shooting, tests, and any other of the recommended trouble shooting options given on YT, Brother's website, and Google fixed that. • It's slow when it comes to adding the cut borders after scanning. So if you have to fiddle with adding or removing colors, you have to wait for it to re-add those borders which adds to the overall time it takes to get to something else that doesn't work properly with this machine. • The machine only accepts Brother approved mats, the mat that comes with it is much to sticky for regular vinyl sticker paper. It didn't accept the second over priced mat I bought from Brother themselves. So there's that. Conclusion: Maybe I just received a faulty machine, but with the sheer amount of problems I've run into trying to use this ScanNCut, I can't imagine that it's simply a faulty machine. The only thing it did well was scan, but everything else was pretty abysmal. To the point where just cutting them manually would have been quicker. Not to mention the waste of materials that went into figuring out all the things that don't work properly with this machine. If you're looking for something to speed up and automate your cutting times, this isn't it. All you will get is an overpriced machine that doesn't work and plenty of options for overpriced accessories that also may or may not work. I will absolutely be returning this and looking for something else.

THIS!!! 🙌 I have a Cricut, a Silhouette Cameo 5 and now this!! This is THE most user friendly, easy to use. I didn’t even need to read the directions. The touch screen has pictures and I was able to read it and figure out what I was needing. This scans whatever you want to cut and just cuts it. Put it on the mat, scan n cut just as advertised. The only thing I will say that another reviewer pointed out was that if you have lettering without a border it will not read the letters and cut correctly. There is a way around that though but not by using the machine. I made templates to cover my design that has letters on the outside and just taped them over top of my design and it read it and cut it. Also the mat is very sticky which is good but it was peeling the back of my sheets off so I had to tape another sheet to the back and then put it on the mat and tape it to the mat for reinforcement. This doesn’t take long and it’s worth it. I was able to trouble shoot and figure out a way around any issues easily unlike my Cricut and Silhouette which troubleshooting can take hours sometimes. This is by far my favorite craft product to date! It works great, it does so much and it’s soooo easy to use. Finally a product easy out of the box!!! Thank you Brother I think I’m in love!

body: The Cricut Explore 4 is extremely user friendly and works like a breeze! Absolutely love the dusty pink color of the Cricut machine, and the size of the machine allows it to fit perfectly in my office! Hardly makes any noise and also cuts so smoothly and evenly. So many different beautifully vibrant colors of iron on vinyls that come with the machine and the color choices make it fun to create many variations of shirts for our business. The Cricut Design Space app has plenty of amazing design collections arranging from beginners all the way to card making. I did have some trouble with the sizing of the letters and characters, but once I realized that I can adjust the height and width at the bottom of the canvas page it's been smooth sailing ever since! 10/10 would recommend the Cricut Explore 4 Machine Kit/ iron press bundle to anyone who is starting or is looking for a different machine for their business or even using the Cricut for personal use. As an extra advantage, I use the iron press at the lowest temperature setting to iron everything.  Lol has made my life easier!

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars
date: Reviewed in the United States on June 2, 2021
body: I FINALLY upgraded my Silhouette 2 after 6 years to the this 4 and was so resistant because of mixed reviews. Now I'm like why didn't I do this sooner?! I am IN LOVE with my new Silhouette Cameo 4!!! CON: 1. So far my *only* negative has been that if my vinyl isn't laying completely flat on the plastic mat while cutting it will make cut marks as the blade drags over it even if no intention to cut that area. As someone who uses mats even when the sticky is gone it means I have to make sure its extra taped down. I didnt have this issue with my 2. 2. There are more nuances and things to mess with and remember but that is because it has more capabilities. I had to watch Youtubes to learn alot of the functions and what needs to be adjusted to what before. PROS: 1. This is worlds faster than my old 2. It can also cut details so much better! I use my Silhouette most to make stencils for glass etching and my designs are often small and detailed. I am beyond excited at its capability! 2. I like the auto adjust blade. I don't have to do anything other than adjust settings in the software. 3. I tried cutting 651 vinyl with no mat and it worked with no issue!!! Make sure to watch tutorials in order to adjust rollers 4. I LOVE that it has a vinyl roll holder!!! I also make signs and have a big wedding order coming up. This will make things so much easier and smoother! 5. I tried print and cut stickers for the first time! I LOVE IT SO MUCH! I was intimidated about it and heard its finicky sometimes but I have cut 20 stickers and not an issue yet. 6. I love the black. Its so sleek! Honestly this machine has been a game changer for growing my small business!

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars
date: Reviewed in the United States on February 27, 2024
body: I love this machine! Its so much easier to use than the Cricut. More features I wont tell about because I dont spoil the surprises! I made my first project using dollar store “vinyl” and it cut it without tearing it. This was worth it. I regret not getting this the first time around. My Cricut wasnt bad, but it was more difficult to use the design space. Plus with the other features this offers, it makes it easier and more of an “all in one machine”! Replacement blades or accessories are not expensive either.

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars

My appreciation to Silhouette America for this product and for Crafter Craft its seller and to Amazon for its shipment. More power to you all!

title: 4.0 out of 5 stars
date: Reviewed in the United States on May 24, 2022
body: Machine cuts great, its done everything i've needed. Finding learning resources was a little bit difficult for certain things like how to print in cmyk which so far doesn't seem possible, but i've been working fine with rgb. The most annoying thing about this though, its time consuming and just extremely frustrating, is the auto register just doesn't work. i've gotten it to work ONE time out of probably a thousand runs now. I've looked online obviously and other solutions such as shining a light on it when its registering, using different printer paper, or adjusting, adding more environmental lights doesn't work for me. I have to manually register it every time. At first manually registering was a pain, couldn't get it to always work, sometimes would try 20 times before it finally registered, but i've found the "sweet spot" now, which is have the cutter blade basically in the bottom right corner of the black square on the paper (so black square is to the left and back slightly of the cutter blade). can get it manually registered without issue every time now. but darn it would sure be nice to have that auto register just work every time.... basically two stars off due to the auto register not working but i really like the machine otherwise so i'm just taking off one star

title: 5.0 out of 5 stars
date: Reviewed in the United States on July 26, 2020
body: I was in a personal tug of war over this and the Cricut models.  I settled on this due to reviews of the software for both companies.  After talking to numerous customers at my local vinyl shop, they too want the Cameo 4 now due to the fact no cutting mat is required (unless it is HTV, I would always use a mat for HTV, and a mat is required if you have small pieces of vinyl you want to use up instead of trashing), it can use rolls of vinyl, has a cutter on the back,  the auto blade is freaking awesome (setting in the program right before you cut and you can test it first before cutting the whole design), and it can cut continuous design up to 10ft long.
